revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Afuchi-jinja]][[ja:安布知神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Afuchi Shrine | native_name = 安布知神社 | image = 安布知神社 参道と大鳥居.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Afuchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.447546|137.745397}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Afuchi Shrine'''|安布知神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11386164|ltq=Q11386164|qid=Q11386164|1=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Oyamada Shrine|lt=Oyamada Shrine|ja|大山田神社|qid=Q85878781|1=Oyamada Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q218813|ltq=Q218813|qid=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Achi, Nagano|lt=Achi|ceb|Achi-mura|en|Achi, Nagano|envoyage|Achi|es|Achi (Nagano)|fa|اچی، ناگانو|fi|Achi|fr|Achi (Nagano)|hy|Ատի (գյուղ)|it|Achi (Giappone)|ja|阿智村|ko|아치촌|nl|Achi (Nagano)|pl|Achi (Nagano)|ru|Ати (село)|tl|Achi, Nagano|tr|Achi, Japonya|uk|Аті (Наґано)|uz|Achi (Nagano)|zh|阿智村|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|lt=Shinano Province|ckb|پارێزگای شینانۆ|cs|Provincie Šinano|de|Provinz Shinano|en|Shinano Province|eo|Provinco Ŝinano|es|Provincia de Shinano|fa|ولایت شینانو|fr|Province de Shinano|gl|Provincia de Shinano|hak|Shinano-koet|id|Provinsi Shinano|it|Shinano (provincia)|ja|信濃国|ko|시나노국|lt|Šinano žemė|ms|Wilayah Shinano|nl|Shinano (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Shinano|pt|Província de Shinano|ru|Синано (провинция)|simple|Shinano Province|sk|Šinano (provincia)|sr|Провинција Шинано|th|แคว้นชินาโนะ|tr|Shinano (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сінано|zh_min_nan|Sinano-kok|zh_yue|信濃國|zh|信濃國|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Q11380911|ltq=Q11380911|qid=Q11380911|1=Ina district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.447546|137.745397}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * 安布知神社 参道と大鳥居.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181970 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7100005009631 == Commons category (P373) == * Afuchi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039913|ltq=Q135039913|qid=Q135039913|1=Achino Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県下伊那郡阿智村駒場2079', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県下伊那郡阿智村智里', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Afuchi-jinja}} [[JA:安布知神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65260948}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Afuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:安布知神社　参道と大鳥居.jpg|270px]]
Main approach and great torii gate |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shimoina District|Q726999|qid=Q726999|1=Shimoina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Achi Village|Q1203279|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}} Komaba 2079 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|26|51.3|N|137|44|42.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=368|Q48000|qid=Q48000|1=368}} (56th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) |Main hall = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Silla Myōjin Shrine (former name) |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Afuchi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|26|51.3|N|137|44|42.9|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|51.3|N|137|44|42.9|E}}}} }} '''Afuchi Shrine''' (あふちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Komaba, {{ill|lt=Achi Village|Q1203279|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shimoina District|Q726999|qid=Q726999|1=Shimoina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}} (Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=368|Q48000|qid=Q48000|1=368}} (56th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}), an eight-petaled mirror was enshrined at Kiyozaka as the sacred proxy of {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}}, and the shrine was founded under the name “Wadō Daijingū.”{{Cite book|author=|title=History of Achi Village|date=1984|year=|accessdate=|publisher=Published by the Achi Village History Editorial Committee|author2=|author3=|author4=|author5=|author6=|author7=|author8=|author9=}} It has long had a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|qid=Q11657447|lt=Achi Shrine|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}}, an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} located several kilometers away. The shrine site is situated on a slope overlooking fields believed to be the site of the ancient {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|qid=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}} “Achi Station.” In {{ill|lt=706|Q23506|qid=Q23506|1=706}} (3rd year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|qid=Q428753|1=Keiun}}), {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) was enshrined together. There was a period when it was known as “Yosaka Hachiman.” In {{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|qid=Q6678|1=1573}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}), the local lord of the {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|qid=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}} enshrined {{ill|lt=Silla Myōjin|Q22127349|qid=Q22127349|1=Shinra Myōjin}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) from {{ill|lt=Onjō-ji|Q1756307|qid=Q1756307|lt=Mii-dera|1=Mii-dera}} in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and the shrine was renamed Silla Myōjin Shrine. {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|qid=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} donated a vermilion-seal land grant of 10 koku under the name Silla Myōjin, and thereafter renewed vermilion-seal documents were issued with each change of shogun. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|qid=Q7610|1=1671}} (11th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}}) by the ruling Miyazaki clan. The main hall is enclosed within a protective structure and is built in the sangensha nagare-zukuri style. The worship hall was built in {{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|qid=Q7624|1=1675}} (3rd year of {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}}) by the shrine priest of the Hayashi family. The ceiling of the worship hall features a central painting of a cloud dragon, surrounded by 144 panel paintings depicting tigers, dragons, carp, and other motifs. In {{ill|lt=1690|Q7678|qid=Q7678|1=1690}} (3rd year of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}}), a stone great torii gate was erected at the entrance to the approach. For a time, the Shingon-sect temple Shinkō-ji stood within the precincts, but it was later abolished and its Buddhist statues were transferred to Chōgaku-ji. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (5th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), the shrine was renamed Afuchi Shrine and became the village shrine of Komaba Village{{refnest|group=Notes|The name Afuchi Shrine had been bestowed in {{ill|lt=1804|Q6894|qid=Q6894|1=1804}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}}) by an imperial edict of the {{ill|lt=Jingikan head|Q17212391|qid=Q17212391|lt=Chief of the Jingikan|1=Chief of the Jingikan}} in Kyoto, but prior to the Meiji period it continued to be commonly known by its former name, Silla Myōjin Shrine.}}. In {{ill|lt=1932|Q18743|qid=Q18743|1=1932}} (7th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}), it was promoted to the rank of gōsha. In {{ill|lt=2015|Q2002|qid=Q2002|1=2015}} (27th year of {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}), the main hall and worship hall were designated as prefectural cultural properties. The shrine treasure, the eight-petaled mirror, was identified by Tomoyasu Nagakubo, head of the Crafts Division of the {{ill|lt=Kyoto National Museum|Q147286|qid=Q147286|1=Kyoto National Museum}}, as a Tang-style mirror from the Nara period known as a “flower-and-bird paired-phoenix eight-petaled mirror.”{{Cite book|author=|title=Rituals of the Mountain Passes of the Tōsandō|date=2008|year=|accessdate=|publisher=Published by ShinSensha|author2=|author3=|author4=|author5=|author6=|author7=|author8=|author9=}} === Shrine grounds === * Ancient holly tree: Designated a natural monument of Achi Village in 1968. Estimated age at the time of designation: 250 years. * Ancient sakaki tree: Designated a natural monument of Achi Village in 1968. Estimated age at the time of designation: 100 years. * Komainu (guardian lion-dogs): On both sides of the stone steps * War memorial monument * Temizuya (purification pavilion) === Image gallery === File:安布知神社 参道と大鳥居.jpg|Main approach and great torii gate File:安布知神社 手水舎.jpg|Purification pavilion File:安布知神社拝殿.jpg|Worship hall File:安布知神社 狛犬ー１.jpg|Komainu 1 File:安布知神社 狛犬ー２.jpg|Komainu 2 File:安布知神社 ヒイラギ古木.jpg|Ancient holly tree File:安布知神社 サカキの古木.jpg|Ancient sakaki tree File:安布知神社拝殿天上図.jpg|Ceiling painting of the worship hall File:安布知神社 戦没者慰霊碑.jpg|War memorial monument File:安布知神社 ハナノキ.jpg|Hanano-ki tree File:安布知神社 花禽双鸞八花鏡.jpg|Flower-and-bird paired-phoenix eight-petaled mirror {{Stub}} === On-site information === ; Location * Komaba 2079, {{ill|lt=Achi Village|Q1203279|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shimoina District|Q726999|qid=Q726999|1=Shimoina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest interchange: About 7 minutes by car (approx. 4.2 km) from the {{ill|lt=Iida-Yamamoto Interchange|Q11666515|qid=Q11666515|1=Iida-Yamamoto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|qid=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === References === * “History of Achi Village,” Achi Village History Editorial Committee, published by the Achi Village History Publication Committee, {{ill|lt=1984|Q2432|qid=Q2432|1=1984}} (Shōwa 59) * “Rituals of the Mountain Passes of the Tōsandō,” by Hidetoshi Ichizawa, published by ShinSensha, {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}} (Heisei 20) === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === Sources === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Afuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Afuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:阿智村の建築物]] [[Category:阿智村の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]
